Okay, awesome. So this one's super short, really simple. As soon as you have the, as soon as they said whatever their problem was, like, you know, I just need to tone up, you know, I just need to get back in shape. Then all you need to do is take that and reiterate back to them and then get to them to assume it. Okay, so you're basically labeling them with a problem. You're sticking that problem saying this is yours now. So they say, you know, I just really need to get back in shape. You're like, awesome. So you try to lose a little bit of weight. What are you trying to do? Like you get even more objective. Like what are you trying to like? Well, because the way the program works is either you lose 20 pounds or sorry, 25 pounds or 6% body fat. Those be okay with you? And they'll be like, yes, I'm like, okay, so you're okay losing 20 pounds or 25 pounds, six weeks, you're okay with that, right? You're gonna come after me and I can be upset with me like, oh man, now I have to buy all new clothes. You know, I'm upset with me about that, right? You can have that joke. They're like, no, I'm not gonna be upset with you. But you're building rapport, but you're also moving the sale forward. Okay, so you clarified why they're there. And then they tell you and then you're making it more specific and assigning it to them and putting a sticker and saying that's your problem now. And now I'm going to tell you how I'm going to solve it. Okay, so pretty cool, really simple, really important. Alright, clarify whether they're assigned into a problem that is going to be relevant to the offer that we have. If you're not selling a challenge, assign them to a problem that is relevant to what you can solve. Alright, because that way it makes the perceived benefit and the solution to the problem really clear, like clear communication. That's how this works. Well, all right, awesome.
